questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a loudspeaker cross-section to check the key physics idea. Why does a loudspeaker cone move backwards and forwards?
options: The cone moves because air pressure charges the coil.
The coil heats up and expands without a force.
The magnet switches off and on by itself.
The current in the coil changes direction, so the force on the coil also changes direction.
questions.correctAnswer: The current in the coil changes direction, so the force on the coil also changes direction.
questions.explanation: Alternating current changes direction, so the motor effect pushes the coil in opposite directions and the cone vibrates.
questions.senNote: Alternating current changes direction, so the motor effect pushes the coil in opposite directions and the cone vibrates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a speaker cone motion explanation to check the key physics idea. Why does a loudspeaker cone move backwards and forwards?
options: The cone moves because air pressure charges the coil.
The coil heats up and expands without a force.
The current in the coil changes direction, so the force on the coil also changes direction.
Gravity pulls the cone backwards and forwards.
questions.correctAnswer: The current in the coil changes direction, so the force on the coil also changes direction.
questions.explanation: Alternating current changes direction, so the motor effect pushes the coil in opposite directions and the cone vibrates.
questions.senNote: Alternating current changes direction, so the motor effect pushes the coil in opposite directions and the cone vibrates.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a generator demonstration to check the key physics idea. What is electromagnetic induction?
options: A conductor becomes a permanent magnet whenever a battery is removed.
A current is made by heating a wire until it glows.
A magnetic field is destroyed when iron is placed near it.
A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.correctAnswer: A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.
questions.senNote: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a moving magnet and coil setup to check the key physics idea. What is electromagnetic induction?
options: A conductor becomes a permanent magnet whenever a battery is removed.
A current is made by heating a wire until it glows.
A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
A potential difference is made only when the conductor is stationary.
questions.correctAnswer: A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.
questions.senNote: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.

The coil produces charge without any energy transfer.

questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.
questions.senNote: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.

questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a scrapyard electromagnet to check the key physics idea. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Remove the iron core.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Increase the current in the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a school solenoid practical to check the key physics idea. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Remove the iron core.
Increase the current in the coil.
Replace the core with plastic.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet

Increase the current in the coil.

questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a scrapyard electromagnet to link the context to the GCSE model. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased? Choose the best answer.
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Increase the current in the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Make the wire insulation thicker only.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a school solenoid practical to link the context to the GCSE model. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Increase the current in the coil.
Use fewer turns on the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Use a non-magnetic aluminium core.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a relay switch example to link the context to the GCSE model. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Reduce the number of cells.
Increase the current in the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a coil-and-core demonstration to link the context to the GCSE model. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Increase the current in the coil.
Put the coil further from the core.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a scrapyard electromagnet to review the evidence in the setup. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Increase the current in the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Reverse the battery once then remove it.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a school solenoid practical to review the evidence in the setup. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased? Choose the best answer.
options: Increase the current in the coil.
Use fewer turns on the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Cool the wire until no current flows.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: A learner uses a relay switch example to review the evidence in the setup. How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased? Choose the best answer.
options: Use fewer turns on the coil.
Decrease the current in the coil.
Use a shorter coil with no turns.
Increase the current in the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a simple motor diagram to check the key physics idea. What causes the motor effect?
options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
A stationary conductor produces no magnetic field and so creates gravity.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a current-carrying wire demo to check the key physics idea. What causes the motor effect?
options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
The force is caused only by heating the wire.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a loudspeaker coil explanation to check the key physics idea. What causes the motor effect?
options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
The wire moves because electrons become protons.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a magnetic force practical to check the key physics idea. What causes the motor effect?
options: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
The field disappears when the current starts.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a simple motor diagram to prepare for an exam-style question. What causes the motor effect? Choose the best answer.
options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
The conductor is pulled only by Earth gravity.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
The motor effect happens without a magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a loudspeaker coil explanation to prepare for an exam-style question. What causes the motor effect?
options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
Current makes the wire radioactive.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics

options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
The force is always zero when current flows.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: A learner uses a simple motor diagram to test a common misconception. What causes the motor effect?
options: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
The wire must be made of plastic to move.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

options: A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a solenoid field pattern to check the key physics idea. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The core removes current from the coil.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a iron-core coil test to check the key physics idea. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
The solenoid becomes an electric insulator.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a electromagnet design note to check the key physics idea. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The field lines disappear inside the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a field-strength comparison to check the key physics idea. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The iron core makes the coil colder only.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a solenoid field pattern to prepare for an exam-style question. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger? Choose the best answer.
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The core turns the current into sound.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a iron-core coil test to prepare for an exam-style question. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger? Choose the best answer.
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
The magnetic domains become completely random.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a electromagnet design note to prepare for an exam-style question. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The field exists only outside the solenoid.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a field-strength comparison to prepare for an exam-style question. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
The core cancels the battery voltage.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: A learner uses a solenoid field pattern to test a common misconception. Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The iron core blocks the magnetic field.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
The field is weaker because iron is non-magnetic.
The turns of wire stop acting together.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
